notes: Manual exemplar rewrite (2026-06-18) to set the quality bar the drafter learns from — same approach as the mythology/wordplay rewrites. Grounded only in the cited Wikipedia lead-extract docs in data/sourcing/docs/sports_games_leisure/.

Calibration: difficulty comes from obliqueness/withholding the obvious, not fact-stacking. Value mix 200→1000 with a genuine hard tail: two deep-cut $800s (Go's 2e170 legal positions; Tour de France clued by L'Auto + the climber/sprinter jerseys with the yellow jersey withheld) and one $1000 (Jim Thorpe via King Gustav V's 'world's greatest athlete' quote — obscure entry to a deep answer).

Subcategory routing uses only the 10 valid sports_games_leisure subcategories; horse racing (Kentucky Derby) and cycling (Tour de France) have no dedicated subcategory so they route to 'Sports Records & Awards'.
Leak hygiene: Backgammon clued by its name-giving triple win without ever using the word; Masters withholds 'green jacket' and 'Augusta'; Tour de France withholds 'yellow jersey'.

questions.clue: Players circle the board in this Depression-era game buying and trading properties named for the streets of Atlantic City, developing them with houses and hotels to drive their rivals into bankruptcy.

citations.title: Monopoly (game)
citations.snippet: Monopoly is a multiplayer economics-themed board game. In the game, players roll two standard dice to move their token clockwise around the game board, buying and trading properties and railroads and developing them with houses and hotels. Players collect rent from their opponents and aim to drive them into bankruptcy. ... The properties on the original game board were named after locations in and around Atlantic City, New Jersey. ... Parker Brothers first published Monopoly in 1935.

questions.clue: Nicknamed 'the Sultan of Swat,' this slugger broke into the majors as a dominant left-handed pitcher for the Boston Red Sox before clubbing a then-record 60 home runs for the 1927 Yankees' 'Murderers' Row.'

citations.title: Babe Ruth
citations.snippet: George Herman "Babe" Ruth was an American professional baseball player whose career in Major League Baseball (MLB) spanned 22 seasons, from 1914 through 1935. Nicknamed "the Bambino" and "the Sultan of Swat", he began his MLB career as a st

questions.clue: The champion of this annual NFL title game hoists the Vince Lombardi Trophy, named for the coach who won its first two editions.

citations.title: Super Bowl
citations.snippet: The Super Bowl is the annual league championship game of the National Football League (NFL) of the United States. It has served as the final game of every NFL season since 1966 replacing the NFL Championship Game and also served as the fina

questions.clue: This two-player game of kings and queens descends from the 7th-century Indian game chaturanga; Wilhelm Steinitz became its first universally recognized world champion in 1886.

citations.title: Chess
citations.snippet: Chess is a board game for two players, played on a square board consisting of 64 squares arranged in an 8×8 grid. The players, referred to as "White" and "Black", each control sixteen pieces: one king, one queen, two rooks, two bishops, two

questions.clue: Devised by out-of-work American architect Alfred Mosher Butts in 1931, this game scores points for words built crossword-style from lettered tiles on a 15-by-15 grid.

citations.title: Scrabble
citations.snippet: Scrabble is a word game in which two to four players score points by placing tiles, each bearing a single letter, onto a game board divided into a 15×15 grid of squares. The tiles must form words that, in crossword fashion, read left to rig

questions.clue: In this family of betting card games, a player can take the entire pot without ever revealing a card — simply by wagering until every opponent folds.

citations.title: Poker
citations.snippet: Poker is a family of comparing card games in which players wager over which hand is best according to that specific game's rules. It is played worldwide, with varying rules in different places. While the earliest known form of the game was

questions.clue: Nicknamed the 'Fall Classic,' this best-of-seven championship has pitted the American League's pennant winner against the National League's nearly every October since 1903.

citations.title: World Series
citations.snippet: The World Series is the annual championship series of Major League Baseball (MLB), a professional baseball league in North America, and has been contested since 1903 between the champion teams of the American League (AL) and the National Le

questions.clue: She stands alone in tennis as the only player to complete a career Golden Slam in both singles and doubles — winning all four majors plus an Olympic gold in each discipline.

citations.title: Serena Williams
citations.snippet: Serena Jameka Williams is an American professional tennis player. She was ranked as the world No. 1 in women's singles by the Women's Tennis Association (WTA) for 319 weeks, and finished as the year-end No. 1 five times. Williams won 73 WTA

questions.clue: This ancient two-player game of dice and counters takes its name from its rarest outcome — a triple-stakes win earned by bearing off all fifteen of your pieces before the opponent removes even one.

citations.title: Backgammon
citations.snippet: Backgammon is a two-player board game played with counters and dice on tables boards. It is the most widespread Western member of the large family of tables games, whose ancestors date back at least 1,600 years. The earliest record of backg

questions.clue: Its winner is draped in a blanket of roses after a run lasting barely two minutes; held every year since 1875, this race for three-year-old Thoroughbreds opens horse racing's Triple Crown.

citations.title: Kentucky Derby
citations.snippet: The Kentucky Derby is an American Grade I stakes race run at Churchill Downs in Louisville, Kentucky. The race is run by three-year-old Thoroughbreds at a distance of 1+1⁄4 miles. Colts and geldings carry 126 pounds and fillies 121 pounds.

questions.clue: With five titles, this is the only national team to have appeared at every FIFA World Cup since the tournament began in 1930.

citations.title: Brazil
citations.snippet: Brazil, officially the Federative Republic of Brazil, is the largest country in South America. It is also the world's fifth-largest country by area and the seventh-largest by population, with over 213 million people. Brazil is a federation

questions.clue: Co-founded by amateur great Bobby Jones and first staged in 1934, this is the only one of golf's four men's majors played on the same course every year.

citations.title: Masters Tournament
citations.snippet: The Masters Tournament is one of the four men's major championships in professional golf. Scheduled for the first full week in April, the Masters is the first major golf tournament of the year. Unlike the other major tournaments which chang

questions.clue: Played with black and white stones on a 19-by-19 grid, this ancient Chinese game has more legal board positions than there are atoms in the observable universe.

citations.title: Go (game)
citations.snippet: Go, Weiqi, or Baduk is an abstract strategy board game for two players in which the aim is to fence off more territory than the opponent. The game was invented in China more than 2,500 years ago and is believed to be the oldest board game c

questions.clue: First organized in 1903 to boost sales of the newspaper L'Auto, this roughly three-week race awards a polka-dot jersey to its best climber and a green jersey to its top sprinter.

citations.title: Tour de France
citations.snippet: The Tour de France is an annual men's multiple-stage road cycling race held primarily in France. It is the oldest and most prestigious of the three Grand Tours, which include the Giro d'Italia and the Vuelta a España.

questions.clue: After this man won the grueling two-day, ten-event decathlon at the 1912 Stockholm Olympics, Sweden's King Gustav V reputedly told him, 'Sir, you are the world's greatest athlete.'

citations.title: Jim Thorpe
